title: fix ssh config parsing for HostName
body:
A later invocation to url.Parse() needs a preceding scheme, otherwise the hostname just gets discarded.

Fixes #25067

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **NOT APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *Jannik2099* **Once this PR has been reviewed and has the lgtm label**, please assign [lsm5](https://github.com/lsm5) for approval. For more information see [the Code Review Process](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process). The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman).
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - **[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)** Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

It's kind of messy to add a test for this, as I'd have to amend the user's `~/.ssh/config`
And judging by the comments in `272-system-connection` , this doesn't run in CI anyways?

Suggestions?
I would suggest at least some simple unit tests.

This is the best I can do, but I am unable to run the original ssh test on my system to begin with.

It's not possible to write a unit test for this as the config parsing functionality is never exposed, it's directly used  in sshClient

Note that these tests don't run in CI either https://cirrus-ci.com/task/5362769018486784

> [+0256s] ok 240 [272] podman system connection - ssh in 322ms # skip Not running under ssh-agent
[+0256s] ok 241 [272] podman system connection - ssh with config in 240ms # skip Not running under ssh-agent
Sorry, I kinda lost interest because I think the way podman parses & passes around ssh configs makes this untestable without refactoring, and that felt silly for a 1 LoC change
We can probably agree to drop the test for this. Let's ask others, WDYT? @containers/podman-maintainers @containers/podman-reviewers

> We can probably agree to drop the test for this. Let's ask others, WDYT? @containers/podman-maintainers @containers/podman-reviewers

+1
I'm fine with merging without tests
